NKJV.1: A Psalm of Solomon. Give the king Your judgments, O God, And Your righteousness to the king’s Son.
NKJV.2: He will judge Your people with righteousness, And Your poor with justice.
NKJV.3: The mountains will bring peace to the people, And the little hills, by righteousness.
NKJV.4: He will bring justice to the poor of the people; He will save the children of the needy, And will break in pieces the oppressor.
NKJV.5: They shall fear You  As long as the sun and moon endure, Throughout all generations.
NKJV.6: He shall come down like rain upon the grass before mowing, Like showers that water the earth.
NKJV.7: In His days the righteous shall flourish,  And abundance of peace, Until the moon is no more.
NKJV.8: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, And from the River to the ends of the earth.
NKJV.9: Those who dwell in the wilderness will bow before Him,  And His enemies will lick the dust.
NKJV.10: The kings of Tarshish and of the isles Will bring presents; The kings of Sheba and Seba Will offer gifts.
NKJV.11: Yes, all kings shall fall down before Him; All nations shall serve Him.
NKJV.12: For He will deliver the needy when he cries, The poor also, and him who has no helper.
NKJV.13: He will spare the poor and needy, And will save the souls of the needy.
NKJV.14: He will redeem their life from oppression and violence; And precious shall be their blood in His sight.
NKJV.15: And He shall live; And the gold of Sheba will be given to Him; Prayer also will be made for Him continually, And daily He shall be praised.
NKJV.16: There will be an abundance of grain in the earth, On the top of the mountains; Its fruit shall wave like Lebanon; And those of the city shall flourish like grass of the earth.
NKJV.17: His name shall endure forever; His name shall continue as long as the sun. And men shall be blessed in Him; All nations shall call Him blessed.
NKJV.18: Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel, Who only does wondrous things!
NKJV.19: And blessed be His glorious name forever! And let the whole earth be filled with His glory. Amen and Amen.
NKJV.20: The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.

KJV.11: Yea, all kings<S>4428</S> shall fall down<S>7812</S> before him: all nations<S>1471</S> shall serve<S>5647</S> him.
KJV.12: For he shall deliver<S>5337</S> the needy<S>34</S> when he crieth<S>7768</S>; the poor<S>6041</S> also, and him that hath no helper<S>5826</S>.
KJV.13: He shall spare<S>2347</S> the poor<S>1800</S> and needy<S>34</S>, and shall save<S>3467</S> the souls<S>5315</S> of the needy<S>34</S>.

KJV.16: There shall be an handful<S>6451</S> of corn<S>1250</S> in the earth<S>776</S> upon the top<S>7218</S> of the mountains<S>2022</S>; the fruit<S>6529</S> thereof shall shake<S>7493</S> like Lebanon<S>3844</S>: and they of the city<S>5892</S> shall flourish<S>6692</S> like grass<S>6212</S> of the earth<S>776</S>.

ESV.1: Give the king your justice, O God, and your righteousness to the royal son!
ESV.2: May he judge your people with righteousness, and your poor with justice!
ESV.3: Let the mountains bear prosperity for the people, and the hills, in righteousness!
ESV.4: May he defend the cause of the poor of the people, give deliverance to the children of the needy, and crush the oppressor!
ESV.5: May they fear you  while the sun endures, and as long as the moon, throughout all generations!
ESV.6: May he be like rain that falls on the mown grass, like showers that water the earth!
ESV.7: In his days may the righteous flourish, and peace abound, till the moon be no more!
ESV.8: May he have dominion from sea to sea, and from the River  to the ends of the earth!
ESV.9: May desert tribes bow down before him, and his enemies lick the dust!
ESV.10: May the kings of Tarshish and of the coastlands render him tribute; may the kings of Sheba and Seba bring gifts!
ESV.11: May all kings fall down before him, all nations serve him!
ESV.12: For he delivers the needy when he calls, the poor and him who has no helper.
ESV.13: He has pity on the weak and the needy, and saves the lives of the needy.
ESV.14: From oppression and violence he redeems their life, and precious is their blood in his sight.
ESV.15: Long may he live; may gold of Sheba be given to him! May prayer be made for him continually, and blessings invoked for him all the day!
ESV.16: May there be abundance of grain in the land; on the tops of the mountains may it wave; may its fruit be like Lebanon; and may people blossom in the cities like the grass of the field!
ESV.17: May his name endure forever, his fame continue as long as the sun! May people be blessed in him, all nations call him blessed!
ESV.18: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who alone does wondrous things.
ESV.19: Blessed be his glorious name forever; may the whole earth be filled with his glory! Amen and Amen!
ESV.20: The prayers of David, the son of Jesse, are ended.

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of Solomon. Give the king Your judgments, O God, And Your righteousness to the king's son.
NASB.2: May he judge Your people with righteousness And Your afflicted with justice.
NASB.3: Let the mountains bring peace to the people, And the hills, in righteousness.
NASB.4: May he vindicate the afflicted of the people, Save the children of the needy And crush the oppressor.
NASB.5: Let them fear You while the sun [endures], And as long as the moon, throughout all generations.
NASB.6: May he come down like rain upon the mown grass, Like showers that water the earth.
NASB.7: In his days may the righteous flourish, And abundance of peace till the moon is no more.
NASB.8: May he also rule from sea to sea And from the River to the ends of the earth.
NASB.9: Let the nomads of the desert bow before him, And his enemies lick the dust.
NASB.10: Let the kings of Tarshish and of the islands bring presents; The kings of Sheba and Seba offer gifts.
NASB.11: And let all kings bow down before him, All nations serve him.
NASB.12: For he will deliver the needy when he cries for help, The afflicted also, and him who has no helper.
NASB.13: He will have compassion on the poor and needy, And the lives of the needy he will save.
NASB.14: He will rescue their life from oppression and violence, And their blood will be precious in his sight;
NASB.15: So may he live, and may the gold of Sheba be given to him; And let them pray for him continually; Let them bless him all day long.
NASB.16: May there be abundance of grain in the earth on top of the mountains; Its fruit will wave like [the cedars of] Lebanon; And may those from the city flourish like vegetation of the earth.
NASB.17: May his name endure forever; May his name increase as long as the sun [shines]; And let [men] bless themselves by him; Let all nations call him blessed.
NASB.18: Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel, Who alone works wonders.
NASB.19: And blessed be His glorious name forever; And may the whole earth be filled with His glory. Amen, and Amen.
NASB.20: The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.

NLT.1:
Give your love of justice to the king, O God,
and righteousness to the king’s son.
NLT.2:
Help him judge your people in the right way;
let the poor always be treated fairly.
NLT.3:
May the mountains yield prosperity for all,
and may the hills be fruitful.
NLT.4:
Help him to defend the poor,
to rescue the children of the needy,
and to crush their oppressors.
NLT.5:
May they fear you as long as the sun shines,
as long as the moon remains in the sky.
Yes, forever!
NLT.6:
May the king’s rule be refreshing like spring rain on freshly cut grass,
like the showers that water the earth.
NLT.7:
May all the godly flourish during his reign.
May there be abundant prosperity until the moon is no more.
NLT.8:
May he reign from sea to sea,
and from the Euphrates River to the ends of the earth.
NLT.9:
Desert nomads will bow before him;
his enemies will fall before him in the dust.
NLT.10:
The western kings of Tarshish and other distant lands
will bring him tribute.
The eastern kings of Sheba and Seba
will bring him gifts.
NLT.11:
All kings will bow before him,
and all nations will serve him.
NLT.12:
He will rescue the poor when they cry to him;
he will help the oppressed, who have no one to defend them.
NLT.13:
He feels pity for the weak and the needy,
and he will rescue them.
NLT.14:
He will redeem them from oppression and violence,
for their lives are precious to him.
NLT.15:
Long live the king!
May the gold of Sheba be given to him.
May the people always pray for him
and bless him all day long.
NLT.16:
May there be abundant grain throughout the land,
flourishing even on the hilltops.
May the fruit trees flourish like the trees of Lebanon,
and may the people thrive like grass in a field.
NLT.17:
May the king’s name endure forever;
may it continue as long as the sun shines.
May all nations be blessed through him
and bring him praise.
NLT.18:
Praise the LORD God, the God of Israel,
who alone does such wonderful things.
NLT.19:
Praise his glorious name forever!
Let the whole earth be filled with his glory.
Amen and amen!
NLT.20: (This ends the prayers of David son of Jesse.)

WEB.1: By Solomon. God, give the king your justice; your righteousness to the royal son.
WEB.2: He will judge your people with righteousness, and your poor with justice.
WEB.3: The mountains shall bring prosperity to the people. The hills bring the fruit of righteousness.
WEB.4: He will judge the poor of the people. He will save the children of the needy, and will break the oppressor in pieces.
WEB.5: They shall fear you while the sun endures; and as long as the moon, throughout all generations.
WEB.6: He will come down like rain on the mown grass, as showers that water the earth.
WEB.7: In his days, the righteous shall flourish, and abundance of peace, until the moon is no more.
WEB.8: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, from the River to the ends of the earth.
WEB.9: Those who dwell in the wilderness shall bow before him. His enemies shall lick the dust.
WEB.10: The kings of Tarshish and of the islands will bring tribute. The kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.
WEB.11: Yes, all kings shall fall down before him. All nations shall serve him.
WEB.12: For he will deliver the needy when he cries; the poor, who has no helper.
WEB.13: He will have pity on the poor and needy. He will save the souls of the needy.
WEB.14: He will redeem their soul from oppression and violence. Their blood will be precious in his sight.
WEB.15: They shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba. Men shall pray for him continually. They shall bless him all day long.
WEB.16: Abundance of grain shall be throughout the land. Its fruit sways like Lebanon. Let it flourish, thriving like the grass of the field.
WEB.17: His name endures forever. His name continues as long as the sun. Men shall be blessed by him. All nations will call him blessed.
WEB.18: Praise be to Yahweh God, the God of Israel, who alone does marvelous deeds.
WEB.19: Blessed be his glorious name forever! Let the whole earth be filled with his glory! Amen and amen.
WEB.20: This ends the prayers by David, the son of Jesse.

CSB17.1:
Of Solomon. ⓕ [1] God, give your justice to the king
and your righteousness to the king’s son. ⓖ
CSB17.2:
He will judge your people with righteousness
and your afflicted ones with justice. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
May the mountains bring well-being [1] to the people
and the hills, righteousness. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
May he vindicate the afflicted among the people,
help the poor,
and crush the oppressor. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
May they fear you [2] while the sun endures
and as long as the moon, throughout all generations. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
May the king be like rain that falls on the cut grass,
like spring showers that water the earth. ⓛ
CSB17.7:
May the righteous [3] flourish in his days
and well-being abound
until the moon is no more. ⓜ
CSB17.8:
May he rule from sea to sea
and from the Euphrates
to the ends of the earth. ⓝ
CSB17.9:
May desert tribes kneel before him
and his enemies lick the dust. ⓐ
CSB17.10:
May the kings of Tarshish
and the coasts and islands bring tribute,
the kings of Sheba and Seba offer gifts. ⓑ
CSB17.11:
Let all kings bow in homage to him,
all nations serve him. ⓒ
CSB17.12:
For he will rescue the poor who cry out
and the afflicted who have no helper. ⓓ
CSB17.13:
He will have pity on the poor and helpless
and save the lives of the poor. ⓔ
CSB17.14:
He will redeem them from oppression and violence,
for their lives are [4] precious [5] in his sight. ⓕ
CSB17.15:
May he live long!
May gold from Sheba be given to him.
May prayer be offered for him continually,
and may he be blessed all day long. ⓖ
CSB17.16:
May there be plenty of grain in the land;
may it wave on the tops of the mountains.
May its crops be like Lebanon.
May people flourish in the cities
like the grass of the field. ⓗ
CSB17.17:
May his name endure forever;
as long as the sun shines,
may his fame increase.
May all nations be blessed by him
and call him blessed. ⓘ
CSB17.18:
Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel,
who alone does wonders. ⓙ
CSB17.19:
Blessed be his glorious name forever;
the whole earth is filled with his glory. ⓚ
Amen and amen.
CSB17.20: The prayers of David son of Jesse are concluded. ⓛ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of Solomon. Give the king [knowledge of] Your judgments, O God,
And [the spirit of] Your righteousness to the king's son [to guide all his ways].
AMP.2:
May he judge Your people with righteousness,
And Your afflicted with justice.
AMP.3:
The mountains will bring peace and prosperity to the people,
And the hills, in [the establishment of] righteousness.
AMP.4:
May he bring justice to the poor among the people,
Save the children of the needy
And crush the oppressor,
AMP.5:
Let them fear You [with awe-inspired reverence and worship You with obedience] while the sun endures,
And as long as the moon [reflects light], throughout all generations.
AMP.6:
May he come down like rain on the mown grass,
Like showers that water the earth.
AMP.7:
In his days may the righteous flourish,
And peace abound until the moon is no more.
AMP.8:
May he also rule from sea to sea
And from the River [Euphrates] to the ends of the earth.
AMP.9:
The nomads of the desert will bow before him,
And his enemies will lick the dust.
AMP.10:
The kings of Tarshish and of the islands will bring offerings;
The kings of Sheba and Seba will offer gifts.
AMP.11:
Yes, all kings will bow down before him,
All nations will serve him.
AMP.12:
For he will rescue the needy when he cries for help,
The afflicted and abused also, and him who has no helper.
AMP.13:
He will have compassion on the poor and needy,
And he will save the lives of the needy.
AMP.14:
He will redeem their life from oppression and fraud and violence,
And their blood will be precious in His sight.
AMP.15:
So may he live, and may the gold of Sheba be given to him;
And let them pray for him continually;
Let them bless and praise him all day long.
AMP.16:
There will be an abundance of grain in the soil on the top of the mountains;
Its fruit will wave like [the cedars of] Lebanon,
And those of the city will flourish like grass of the earth.
AMP.17:
May his name endure forever;
May his name continue as long as the sun;
And let men bless themselves by him;
Let all nations call him blessed.
AMP.18:
Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel,
Who alone does wonderful things.
AMP.19:
Blessed be His glorious name forever;
And may the whole earth be filled with His glory.
Amen and Amen.
AMP.20: The prayers of David son of Jesse are ended.

MSG.1:
A Solomon psalm
Give the gift of wise rule to the king, O God,
the gift of just rule to the crown prince.
MSG.2:
May he judge your people rightly,
be honorable to your meek and lowly.
MSG.3:
Let the mountains give exuberant witness;
shape the hills with the contours of right living.
MSG.4:
Please stand up for the poor,
help the children of the needy,
come down hard on the cruel tyrants.
MSG.5:
Outlast the sun, outlive the moon—
age after age after age.
MSG.6:
Be rainfall on cut grass,
earth-refreshing rain showers.
MSG.7:
Let righteousness burst into blossom
and peace abound until the moon fades to nothing.
MSG.8:
Rule from sea to sea,
from the River to the Rim.
MSG.9:
Foes will fall on their knees before God,
his enemies lick the dust.
MSG.10:
Kings remote and legendary will pay homage,
kings rich and resplendent will turn over their wealth.
MSG.11:
All kings will fall down and worship,
and godless nations sign up to serve him,
MSG.12:
Because he rescues the poor at the first sign of need,
the destitute who have run out of luck.
MSG.13:
He opens a place in his heart for the down-and-out,
he restores the wretched of the earth.
MSG.14:
He frees them from tyranny and torture—
when they bleed, he bleeds;
when they die, he dies.
MSG.15:
And live! Oh, let him live!
Deck him out in Sheba gold.
Offer prayers unceasing to him,
bless him from morning to night.
MSG.16:
Fields of golden grain in the land,
cresting the mountains in wild exuberance,
Cornucopias of praise, praises
springing from the city like grass from the earth.
MSG.17:
May he never be forgotten,
his fame shine on like sunshine.
May all godless people enter his circle of blessing
and bless the One who blessed them.
MSG.18:
Blessed God, Israel's God,
the one and only wonder-working God!
MSG.19:
Blessed always his blazing glory!
All earth brims with his glory.

NIV.1:
Psalm 72
Of Solomon.
Endow the king with your justice, O God,
the royal son with your righteousness.
NIV.2:
He will judge your people in righteousness,
your afflicted ones with justice.
NIV.3:
The mountains will bring prosperity to the people,
the hills the fruit of righteousness.
NIV.4:
He will defend the afflicted among the people
and save the children of the needy;
he will crush the oppressor.
NIV.5:
He will endure as long as the sun,
as long as the moon, through all generations.
NIV.6:
He will be like rain falling on a mown field,
like showers watering the earth.
NIV.7:
In his days the righteous will flourish;
prosperity will abound till the moon is no more.
NIV.8:
He will rule from sea to sea
and from the River to the ends of the earth.
NIV.9:
The desert tribes will bow before him
and his enemies will lick the dust.
NIV.10:
The kings of Tarshish and of distant shores
will bring tribute to him;
the kings of Sheba and Seba
will present him gifts.
NIV.11:
All kings will bow down to him
and all nations will serve him.
NIV.12:
For he will deliver the needy who cry out,
the afflicted who have no one to help.
NIV.13:
He will take pity on the weak and the needy
and save the needy from death.
NIV.14:
He will rescue them from oppression and violence,
for precious is their blood in his sight.
NIV.15:
Long may he live!
May gold from Sheba be given him.
May people ever pray for him
and bless him all day long.
NIV.16:
Let grain abound throughout the land;
on the tops of the hills may it sway.
Let its fruit flourish like Lebanon;
let it thrive like the grass of the field.
NIV.17:
May his name endure forever;
may it continue as long as the sun.
All nations will be blessed through him,
and they will call him blessed.
NIV.18:
Praise be to the Lord God, the God of Israel,
who alone does marvelous deeds.
NIV.19:
Praise be to his glorious name forever;
may the whole earth be filled with his glory.
Amen and Amen.
NIV.20: This concludes the prayers of David son of Jesse.

NRSVCE.1:
Give the king your justice, O God,
and your righteousness to a king’s son.
NRSVCE.2:
May he judge your people with righteousness,
and your poor with justice.
NRSVCE.3:
May the mountains yield prosperity for the people,
and the hills, in righteousness.
NRSVCE.4:
May he defend the cause of the poor of the people,
give deliverance to the needy,
and crush the oppressor.
NRSVCE.5:
May he live while the sun endures,
and as long as the moon, throughout all generations.
NRSVCE.6:
May he be like rain that falls on the mown grass,
like showers that water the earth.
NRSVCE.7:
In his days may righteousness flourish
and peace abound, until the moon is no more.
NRSVCE.8:
May he have dominion from sea to sea,
and from the River to the ends of the earth.
NRSVCE.9:
May his foes bow down before him,
and his enemies lick the dust.
NRSVCE.10:
May the kings of Tarshish and of the isles
render him tribute,
may the kings of Sheba and Seba
bring gifts.
NRSVCE.11:
May all kings fall down before him,
all nations give him service.
NRSVCE.12:
For he delivers the needy when they call,
the poor and those who have no helper.
NRSVCE.13:
He has pity on the weak and the needy,
and saves the lives of the needy.
NRSVCE.14:
From oppression and violence he redeems their life;
and precious is their blood in his sight.
NRSVCE.15:
Long may he live!
May gold of Sheba be given to him.
May prayer be made for him continually,
and blessings invoked for him all day long.
NRSVCE.16:
May there be abundance of grain in the land;
may it wave on the tops of the mountains;
may its fruit be like Lebanon;
and may people blossom in the cities
like the grass of the field.
NRSVCE.17:
May his name endure forever,
his fame continue as long as the sun.
May all nations be blessed in him;
may they pronounce him happy.
NRSVCE.18:
Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel,
who alone does wondrous things.
NRSVCE.19:
Blessed be his glorious name forever;
may his glory fill the whole earth. Amen and Amen.
NRSVCE.20: The prayers of David son of Jesse are ended.

NET.1: O God, grant the king the ability to make just decisions! Grant the king’s son the ability to make fair decisions!
NET.2: Then he will judge your people fairly, and your oppressed ones equitably.
NET.3: The mountains will bring news of peace to the people, and the hills will announce justice.
NET.4: He will defend the oppressed among the people; he will deliver the children of the poor and crush the oppressor.
NET.5: People will fear you as long as the sun and moon remain in the sky, for generation after generation.
NET.6: He will descend like rain on the mown grass, like showers that drench the earth.
NET.7: During his days the godly will flourish; peace will prevail as long as the moon remains in the sky.
NET.8: May he rule from sea to sea, and from the Euphrates River to the ends of the earth!
NET.9: Before him the coastlands will bow down, and his enemies will lick the dust.
NET.10: The kings of Tarshish and the coastlands will offer gifts; the kings of Sheba and Seba will bring tribute.
NET.11: All kings will bow down to him; all nations will serve him.
NET.12: For he will rescue the needy when they cry out for help, and the oppressed who have no defender.
NET.13: He will take pity on the poor and needy; the lives of the needy he will save.
NET.14: From harm and violence he will defend them; he will value their lives.
NET.15: May he live! May they offer him gold from Sheba! May they continually pray for him! May they pronounce blessings on him all day long!
NET.16: May there be an abundance of grain in the earth; on the tops of the mountains may it sway! May its fruit trees flourish like the forests of Lebanon! May its crops be as abundant as the grass of the earth!
NET.17: May his fame endure! May his dynasty last as long as the sun remains in the sky! May they use his name when they formulate their blessings! May all nations consider him to be favored by God!
NET.18: The LORD God, the God of Israel, deserves praise! He alone accomplishes amazing things!
NET.19: His glorious name deserves praise forevermore! May his majestic splendor fill the whole earth! We agree! We agree!
NET.20: This collection of the prayers of David son of Jesse ends here.
